creator: obrien@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, Sep 20, 2006 at 07:39:26PM +0400, Dmitry Marakasov wrote:
 > That was erroneous, because OPTIONS should be placed before including bsd.port.pre.mk, so now OPTIONS don't work correctly for this port.
 
 It [seemed to] work in my testing.   Can you please help me see how it
 doesn't work?
  
 > I have two different fixes for this. First one simple removes conditional and makes PLUGIN disabled by default,
 
 > when second uses MACHINE_ARCH instead of ARCH to determine architecture, thus enabling us to place OPTIONS before bsd.port.pre.mk.
 
 I used MACHINE_ARCH in rev 1.8, but Kris asked me to change it to just
 ARCH.  I guess we have go back to rev 1.8's way.  It would be helpful to
 understand the problem you mention above to know why we have to break the
 "ARCH not MACHINE_ARCH" rule.
 
 > I myself prefer patch1, as I don't think OPTIONS are meant for such handling.
 
 Please suggest a patch that achieves the goal.
  
 > -.include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
 > -
 > -.if ${ARCH} == "amd64"
 > +.if ${MACHINE_ARCH} == "amd64"
 
 "ARCH" is the correct spelling per the main FreeBSD Portmanager.
 
 >  OPTIONS=	PLUGIN	"Enable firefox plugin" on
 >  .else
 >  OPTIONS=	PLUGIN	"Enable firefox plugin" off
 >  .endif
 > +
 > +.include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
 
 One cannot do this change as ARCH isn't available until after
 bsd.port.pre.mk is included.
  
 -- 
 -- David  (obrien@FreeBSD.org)
